messages.content:
I can’t run tasks from here. I need a bit more detail about your project and environment so I can give exact commands. Quick reasoning: “task 3” could be defined in many different test harnesses (k6, Locust, JMeter, Artillery, a CI workflow, Shipyard/Makefile script, etc.), so I’ll give common options — tell me which one matches your repo and I’ll give the exact command or script.

If you want me to run it locally, say so and provide the test file / command name. Otherwise pick the scenario below that matches your setup:

1) k6
- Typical command (from repo root):
  k6 run path/to/task3.js
- With overrides:
  k6 run --vus 100 --duration 30s path/to/task3.js

2) Locust (headless)
- Typical command:
  locust -f locustfile.py --headless -u 100 -r 10 -t 1m --only-summary
- To run a specific task/class, set LOCUST_LOCATOR or import/execute that class in locustfile.

3) JMeter
- Typical non-GUI run:
  jmeter -n -t path/to/task3.jmx -l results_task3.jtl -j jmeter_task3.log

4) Artillery
- Typical command:
  artillery run path/to/task3.yml

5) GitHub Actions / CI
- Trigger the workflow/run in the Actions UI, or via CLI:
  gh workflow run <workflow_file.yml> --ref main
- If task 3 is a job in the workflow, ensure workflow is callable with inputs or push a branch/PR that triggers it.

6) Makefile / npm script / custom script
- Make target:
  make task3
- npm script:
  npm run task3
- yarn:
  yarn task3

What to tell me so I can give the exact command or steps:
- Which load test tool / test file is used (k6, Locust, JMeter, Artillery, a Make/npm script, or a CI workflow).
- The repository path or file name for “task 3” (e.g., scripts/task3.js, tests/load/task3.yml).
- Where you want it run (local terminal, CI, Docker container, Kubernetes, Shipyard UI).

If you want me to change/add test code or CI config to make task 3 runnable, submit again with a concrete coding request (or turn off Chat-only mode).

nextActions.label: Continue chat
nextActions.description: Ask a follow-up question in the same thread.
